titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/226

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=180 status=live modified=2021-04-05 09:09:15.819731+00 | translation moa=181 status=live modified=2021-04-05 09:09:17.413724+00

div.p:
Herb’ . Camerarius Regis Scot’ . Vniuersis catholice Matris Ecclesie filiis salutem. Notum sit
uobis me concessisse ⁊ mea carta confirmasse Ecclesie sancte crucis de castro puellarum ⁊ Cano
nicis ibidem deo seruientibus Ecclesiam de Kinel cum omnibus rectitudinibus suis . in decimis .
in terris . in bosco ⁊ plano . in pascuis ⁊ pratis . in perpetuam Elemosinam . consensu ⁊ consilio filiorum meorum
Stephani ⁊ Willelmi . Volo igitur ut predicti Canonici prenominatam Ecclesiam ita libere ⁊ quiete
possideant  sicut aliqua Ecclesia in tota Lodonia liberius ⁊ quietius possidetur . Salua tenore
domini Willelmi Nepotis mei  quam diu prenominatam Ecclesiam tenere uoluerit . Testibus
... Hugone Giffard . Terrico Nepote meo ⁊ Petro . ⁊ Ric’ Clerico . Apud Kinel .

Herbert, Chamberlain of the king of Scots, to all sons of Catholic Mother Church, Greeting: Be it known to you that I have granted and by my charter made firm to the church of Holyrood at Maidens’ Castle and to the canons serving God there the church of Kinneil with all its rights, in teinds, in lands, in wood and plain, in pastures and meadows, in perpetual alms, by the consent and counsel of my sons Stephen and William. I therefore will that the aforesaid canons possess the aforenamed church as freely and quietly as any church in the whole of Lothian is freely and quietly possessed. Reserving the tenure of William my nephew, for as long as he should wish to hold the aforenamed church. As witnesses ... Hugh Giffard, Terry my nephew, and Peter, and Richard the clerk. At Kinneil.
